title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot visits Miss Morley to discuss her brother Henry’s death. Miss Morley explains that his professional papers were handled by Miss Neville and his patients were distributed among successors like Mr. Ralegh. She expresses contempt for the murder of Miss Sainsbury-Silas, a patient Henry had not previously mentioned. When Poirot asks about a patient named Mrs. Chapman, Miss Morley directs him to Miss Neville, who is now working at a dentist's in Ramstead. Miss Morley also expresses her dislike for Frank Carter, suggesting he lacks moral principles and has a short temper, though she sees no motive for him to kill Henry.

During the visit, Poirot observes Agnes, a former maid who now serves as a cook. As she departs, Agnes nervously asks if the death was truly a suicide caused by a dosage mistake, noting that Miss Morley disagrees. Poirot senses Agnes is hiding something. He recalls the layout of the Morley residence at 58 Charlotte Street, noting that the police ruled out outsiders because the house was sealed, with the only entrance being the front door managed by Alfred.

Later, Mr. Barnes visits Poirot. He reveals that Albert Chapman (also referred to as Albert Chalmers) was a spy with the code name Q.X.912, whose job involved traveling to various locations under believable pretexts. Barnes mentions rumors of Chapman's death but remains skeptical. Finally, Japp meets with Poirot and expresses a theory that the corpse found is not actually Miss Sainsbury-Silas, suggesting the victim's face was destroyed to hide her identity. Japp believes Henry Morley was killed specifically to prevent him from testifying.
